intro: Rostellularia prostrata, also known as the creeping rattle-pod or creeping rattle-pod, is a perennial plant native to Australia, characterized by its sprawling habit and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rostellularia prostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Australian herbalism, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, or dried and used as a powder to make capsules or tablets.
